summary: Iteration 155 — Multi-phone support for customers. 32/33 backend tests pass. All UI features verified. Found 1 critical bug (secondary phone inbox not matched to customer), 1 minor gap (thread header missing phones without messages).

critical.issue: Customer lookup uses only primary `phone` field (singular), NOT the `phones` array. Incoming SMS from a secondary phone (not the primary phone) is stored as registered=False, customer_id='' (unknown). Example: MARIA SANTOS has phones=['09951112222','09953334444'] but phone='09951112222'. SMS from '09953334444' is not recognized as registered.
critical.fix: Change customer lookup in receive_inbox_sms, check_phone, and sent_from_device to also check phones array: {"$or": [{"phone": {"$in": phones}}, {"phones": {"$in": phones}}]}

minor.issue: Response phones[] only includes phones that appear in messages, not ALL registered phones from customer record. Phone '09957776666' (registered but no messages) is missing from thread header even though it's in customer.phones array. Feature says 'Thread header shows ALL customer phone numbers'.
minor.fix: Return all_phones (which already includes cust_phones) instead of message-derived phones: change `sorted(p for p in set(m.get('phone','') for m in all_msgs) if p)` to `sorted(p for p in all_phones if p)` at line ~888

minor.issue: From iteration_153 carry-over: blast endpoint still uses old 'business_info' key (line 474) instead of 'company_info'.

integration_issues.flow: Customers page — conversation list shows TEST_MULTIPHONEUSER twice
integration_issues.issue: Leftover test data from multiple test runs created duplicate sms_queue records with different customer_ids for same name. NOT a code bug — just test data artifact. Real customer (MARIA SANTOS) appears once correctly.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix receive_inbox_sms, check_phone, sent_from_device in sms.py to also check phones[] array for secondary phone matching. Current query: {"phone": {"$in": phones}}. Required: {"$or": [{"phone": {"$in": phones}}, {"phones": {"$in": phones}}]}
MINOR: Fix thread endpoint to return all_phones (including phones with no messages) instead of only message-derived phones
CARRY-OVER: Fix blast endpoint company_info key (iteration_153)
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: sms.py receive_inbox_sms (line ~677): customer lookup does {"phone": {"$in": phones}} which checks only the primary phone field. Customers with secondary phones in phones[] array won't be recognized when SMS arrives from those secondary numbers. This creates the exact problem the multi-phone feature was meant to solve.
BUG: sms.py check_phone (line ~579): same issue — only checks primary phone field. Android gateway whitelist check will also fail for secondary phones.
MINOR: conversation/customer/{id} endpoint returns phones derived from messages only, not all registered phones. A phone registered but never used in messages is silently excluded.
PASS: assign_phone_to_customer correctly uses $addToSet (not replace). Verified.
PASS: send_manual_sms correctly queries customer.phones and iterates all phones when customer_id given.
PASS: sms_hooks.py all 3 hooks (credit_new, payment_received, charge_applied) iterate phones array correctly.
PASS: conversations list correctly groups by customer_id (not per-phone).
PASS: customers.py all CRUD endpoints (create, update, add_phone, remove_phone) work correctly.

success_rate.backend: 97% (32/33 tests passed — 1 test data ordering issue with MARIA SANTOS lowercase vs uppercase)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_MULTIPHONEUSER customer with phones [09880001001, 09880001002] during tests — deleted after suite. Posted inbox messages from 09880001002 (secondary phone) and 09880001099. Some test data may persist in sms_queue/sms_inbox.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-phone feature mostly working. Critical bug: sms.py receive_inbox_sms/check_phone/sent_from_device only check primary phone field, not phones[] array. Secondary phone inboxes go to unknown section. MARIA SANTOS (id:5817ed81-524d-451c-9536-51427e6a500c) has 3 phones: ['09951112222','09953334444','09957776666']. Thread shows only 2 phones (those with messages). Conversations list correctly groups by customer_id. assign-phone correctly uses $addToSet. UI: Customer form Add number button works, thread header shows phones, reply hint shows. Branch 1: c435277f-9fc7-4d83-83e7-38be5b4423ac.

mocked_apis.note: Physical SMS dispatch is MOCKED — messages are queued but not actually sent via Android gateway.

rca_of_critical_bug.bug: Incoming SMS from secondary phones not recognized as registered customer
rca_of_critical_bug.root_cause: In sms.py receive_inbox_sms (~line 677), the customer lookup query is: {"phone": {"$in": phones}} where 'phones' is a list of variants of the INCOMING phone number (e.g., ['09880001002', '+639880001002']). This checks if the customer's primary 'phone' field equals the incoming number. However, for multi-phone customers, the secondary phones are stored in the 'phones' ARRAY field, not in the 'phone' field. So secondary phone lookups fail.
rca_of_critical_bug.reproduction: 1. Create customer with phones=['09880001001','09880001002']; 2. POST /sms/inbox with phone='09880001002'; 3. Response shows registered=False, customer_id='' — should be registered=True
rca_of_critical_bug.fix: Change all 3 customer lookup queries in sms.py (lines ~677, ~619, ~580) from {"phone": {"$in": phones}} to {"$or": [{"phone": {"$in": phones}}, {"phones": {"$in": phones}}]}

tests_performed.test: POST /customers with phones[] array stores all phones
Primary phone (phone field) set to first in phones array
GET /customers/{id} persists phones array
PUT /customers/{id} with phones[] updates correctly
POST /customers/{id}/phones adds new phone
POST /customers/{id}/phones duplicate is no-op
POST /customers/{id}/phones empty phone → 400
DELETE /customers/{id}/phones/{phone} removes phone
POST /sms/send with customer_id queues to 2 phones → {queued: 2}
POST /sms/send missing message → 400
GET /sms/conversations groups by customer_id (not per-phone)
GET /sms/conversation/customer/{id} returns 200 with expected fields
Unified thread includes sent messages from both phones
PATCH /sms/assign-phone returns 200 with migrated_messages
PATCH /sms/assign-phone ADDS phone to array (phones[] grows)
PATCH /sms/assign-phone preserves existing primary phone
PATCH /sms/assign-phone missing params → 400
PATCH /sms/assign-phone bad customer → 404
Inbox from secondary phone - BUG: stored as registered=False
UI: Customer form shows Phone Numbers section with + Add number button
UI: Second phone input appears after clicking + Add number
UI: First phone has Primary label
UI: Save customer with 2 phones → Customer created toast
UI: Customers table shows comma-separated phones for MARIA SANTOS
UI: Thread header shows phones for multi-phone customer
UI: Reply box shows 'Replying to 2 numbers' hint
UI: MARIA SANTOS (multi-phone) appears as ONE entry in conversation list
